title: Project Report: Multi-Source Finance Model — Power BI Analyst
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: Merge SQL Server + Excel + API data; complex DAX time intelligence; row-level security by department.
objectives: Merge 3 data sources in Power Query
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Intermediate)*

**Multi-Source Finance Model** is a intermediate-level end-to-end project for Power BI Analyst. Duration: 3–4 weeks. Merge SQL Server + Excel + API data; complex DAX time intelligence; row-level security by department. Tools: Power BI, DAX Studio, SQL Server.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: Merge 3 data sources in Power Query
Objective: Implement RLS by department
Objective: YTD/QoQ DAX measures
Objective: Performance tune with DAX Studio
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Power BI Analyst portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Power BI Analyst resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Power BI Analyst resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: ETL
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: ETL** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Power Query merge queries. Complete these tasks: API connector; SQL direct query. Deliverable for this phase: Combined dataset.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] API connector
- [ ] SQL direct query
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase ETL is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it
During week 1 of the project timeline.

### Where to use it
Power BI Analyst agile sprints and coursework milestones.

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Combined dataset to the teacher.

chapters.title: Phase: RLS
chapters.content:

```text
- [ ] Test roles
```

chapters.title: Phase: Time Intel
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: Time Intel** *(Level: Intermediate)*

YTD, YoY, rolling averages. Complete these tasks: DATEADD; SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR. Deliverable for this phase: Finance KPI page.

Code Reference:
```text

- [ ] SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR
```

chapters.content:

```text
- [ ] Variables
```

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Intermediate)*

Submit all final deliverables: PBIX; RLS documentation; Performance comparison; User guide. Skills demonstrated: Power Query, DAX, RLS, Performance. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: PBIX
Deliverable: RLS documentation
Deliverable: Performance comparison
Deliverable: User guide
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits PBIX and passes the rubric.
